subject: Decouple configuration from application
commit_message:
Decouple configuration from application

- Decouple configuration from application
- Generate all 3 types of docker variations

Issue-ID: CPS-175
Signed-off-by: Claudio D. Gasparini <claudio.gasparini@pantheon.tech>
Change-Id: I1e2e0577c5911f7c79801e4c691d196515dc02a1

entries.message:

Ruslan can you tomorrow please test all 3 scenarios using generated dockers images and docker-compose

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

With this config, I'm not able to start the application straight in the ide anymore.

Are you still able to run the application without building the docker image and without docker compose ?

I would like to ensure that there is no regression here.

Patch Set 6:

> Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1
> 
> With this config, I'm not able to start the application straight in the ide anymore.
> 
> Are you still able to run the application without building the docker image and without docker compose ?
> 
> I would like to ensure that there is no regression here.

this statement is incorrect. 
i can confirm the application can be started from ide directly (idea)
but it requires a required profile enabled in order to provide required
dependencies (application uses no other modules by default) 
any of listed will do the job: cps-docker, xnf-docker, cps-xnf-docker

Patch Set 6:

> Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1
> 
> With this config, I'm not able to start the application straight in the ide anymore.
> 
> Are you still able to run the application without building the docker image and without docker compose ?
> 
> I would like to ensure that there is no regression here.

I agree, without docker, the application is not building now with this patch. And that should not be the case. By default, the application should be able to successfully do a maven build and when the docker profiles are specified, the docker build should be done.

Patch Set 6:

> Patch Set 6:
> 
> > Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > With this config, I'm not able to start the application straight in the ide anymore.
> >

> > 
> > I would like to ensure that there is no regression here.
> 
> I agree, without docker, the application is not building now with this patch. And that should not be the case. By default, the application should be able to successfully do a maven build and when the docker profiles are specified, the docker build should be done.

you can build without docker, use -Djib.skip

> any of listed will do the job: cps-docker, xnf-docker, cps-xnf-docker

I was not saying that it does not work, I was mentioning that I was not able to make it run, and would like to ensure it is still possible to run the application locally within the ide without building the docker image upfront.
